author: Michael OKeefe
title: Life changing info for kidney patients
body: Lee Hull has written the definitive book for understanding kidney disease. Each page is full of important information for patients and doctors alike. This is such a frustrating disease with so many moving parts. For us non-medical types, some of the information Is so technical that I have to have the dictionary near by. But it's always worth it. I read some every day. I use for a reference, and I make questions for my nephrologist before each visit. So far it has helped me stabilize my gfr and to take some prophylactic measures before other symptoms begin. Thank you, Lee Hull, for this book.

author: Donna Doellinger
title: Very helpful
body: Good book

author: Amazon Customer
title: Basic knowledge
body: Way, way to technical..,

title: Let Science Be Your Guide To Controllimg Your Kidneys
body: After a nephrologist told me that in three years, I was going on dialysis, I looked elsewhere and found this book. My GFR scores went from 42 to 60.7 by altering my diet, based on the recommendations and science from this book. Diet is key.

author: Arielle A.
title: You can save your kidneys if you’re serious. Read this book!!!
body: Mr. Hull is brilliant and has put mind-breaking effort into identifying the variables that affect kidneys, researching hundreds and hundreds of abstracts concerning them (thousands maybe?)) collating the data, and drawing scientific conclusions in each case that diet will help and not hurt, the whole point of the book. Diet affects kidney disease, something the medical world routinely dismisses. This book is groundbreaking inasmuch as there is NO OTHER RESOURCE like it. Over a two month period my GFR had descended from 41 to 38, and at that rate I would be looking at dialysis in about 7 months. I was frantic. My docs basically told me to eat less red meat and try to take fewer NSAIDS, (aspirin, ibuprofen etc). I had no meat in my diet and took no NSAIDS. So where did that leave me! Desperate, that’s where. I found Stopping Kidney Disease online, accessed the book, read each chapter carefully and extrapolated the information into an aggressive diet of no meat, no fish, no eggs, no dairy and no grains. (A little bit of fudging with dairy and grains). I was praying that if I went that extreme, that in two months my GFR might return to 41. I also also pulled the PRAL chart so I could calculate my datily renal acid load for each food, and I calculated the protein grams for each food consumed. It was simple math at the end of the day. There are other factors but these are the two biggies. It was all about whether my renal acid load was in the negative, and whether my my protein grams consumed were 30 or less. A helluva learning curve, but I did it, Was it extreme? Yes, but then I remembered my priorities. Dialysis is what’s extreme and that’s what I was headed for and I was terrified. Because of this diet, in two months, my GFR soared from 38 to 56 and my doctors jaws literally dropped. Now I’m seeing what it will take diet-wise to either maintain that number or keep ascending. Sort of a mathematical game at this point. How about 90-100 GFR? Today the world, tomorrow the galaxy! Veronica

author: Sally Jones-McNamara, Senior Healthcare Compliance Executive
title: Every CKD pt. and every Nephrology office needs this text
body: Exceptionally well done text for Patients w Kidney Disease! I began reading pre publication chapters last year and stopped eating red meat; ate mostly veg/fruit; lost 45 lbs. my eGFR jumped from 32 to 44 to 46 to 56 in Dec.2018. I got Pneumonia Christmas Day and was very ill for about 7 weeks. Had two rounds of antibiotics and Prednisone. Gained 9 lbs. in 7 days from the Steroids; + 17 lbs. total by 3/1/19; severe Anemia required two Fereheme 510 infusions. Hemoglobin went from 4 to 10; I was not able to follow renal diet while so illl. Grrrr... eGFR dropped to 49. Bought this reference guide as soon as it was available. I am back on track; study this text daily and fully expect to be able to get my eGFR back up to 56 and higher. Purchased Albutrix and will begin taking it next week to be sure I am meeting my CHON needs. Every patient diagnosed w CKD and every medical student aiming for BdCert in Nephrology needs this text! It has been more helpful than all of the other resources I have read or researched or received from EVERY OTHER SOURCE, including my Nephrologist, who is excellent. I plan to take my copy w me to my next appointment with him. Lee Hull has compiled a wonderful resource guide in this text. I am anxious to buy his next book ( expecked to be out next week); I have heard it contains recipes and menu plans. I am very grateful to Lee Hull for the incredible effort he has put into his work. I respect his research.

author: cathy shuback
title: Every person with kidney disease must have this book.
body: This book is a must for kidney patients and their doctors. It gives me some hope. I am just starting the process, but finally an updated book with tons of valuable information that will help me slow down my disease. Thank you Lee Hull for all your research and time developing this book that I know will help many people. You are a blessing to all people with Kidney disease.

title: A must have for anyone with CKD!
body: This book is a goldmine of information. Like Lee, my doctors told me to carry on as I was when I was told I had stage 3 CKD. Like most people I wasn’t content with this, and the zero information I was given. I researched, But the more I read, the more confused I was. As I have type 2 diabetes I was especially confused, as I seemed I shouldn’t eat white or brown bread! So I gave up and decided to get on with my life, eating what I wanted with care checking the ingredients. Reading Lee’s book week by week online has given me a sense of direction, and the reassuring knowledge that I was not alone in feeling that doctors don’t have a clue - don’t need to do anything until you’re stage 4? I have a few ideas for the way forward now, so I am excited that the book is now available in a traditional format that I can pull these threads together and start on a plan. Thank you Lee for what you have done. You have written about something incredibly complex in a most readable and understandable way, a remarkable achievement!

author: Leslie D.
title: Captivating and informative!
body: Lee's writing is totally transformational. It denotes an emergent feeling of ultimate triumph. The portion of the book where he discuss renal hypoxia was of most importance to me personally, as my daughter appears to have those symptoms, and I was able to mention it to my daughter's nephrologist who appeared no to know what I was talking about. He did not remember what renal Hypoxia is and looked at me in complete surprise. Stopping Kidney Disease is very educational and helpful. Thank you Lee so very much for such an extraordinaire work. Good bless your wisdom!

author: Amazon Customer
title: The sooner you get on board with this information, the greater your chances with kidney disease.
body: This is a MUST read for anyone with kidney disease. It has an abundance of information, backed by research, to assist the patient with the challenges of this disease. My husband (the patient) and I have hope we can at least slow down this progression and at best put this in remission.

author: Louis Floyd
title: Stopping Kidney Disease.. is a absolute MUST READ
body: Best book on kidney disease ever! Buy the paperback and don't try to keep the downloaded chapters in order, it's too hard. Paperback has everything in one place. I learned more from the downloads than my doctor, nephrologist and nutritionist combined. Like the others said, not easy, but I am already seeing improvements.

author: Mar
title: Life changing!
body: Lee Hull is a wonderful man who is sharing his experience in kidney health. I started with him a while ago. While I do not have kidney disease, but I have damaged kidneys from being poisoned (same as some medical treatments). I was constantly sick with major kidney infections. When I followed his chapters and changed my eating and watched anything that stresses the kidneys, I have been free of kidney infections for almost a year now. The book is worth so much more than the cost.

author: hitchicken
title: This book and author is changing my blood numbers.
body: If you suffer kidney disease, read this book. It's long. Some parts are easy to read and understand. Some parts are referenced historical case studies that would put a bear into hibernation. But the content is a revelation. I started downloading chapters back in mid-December, 2018. Following Hull's advice is very hard (psychologically). The diet is very, very hard. I did it. In a month, my eGFR went from 35 to 40. My potassium from 5.6 to 5.2 (It's been as high as 7.2 - ended up in ER). My glucose, down dramatically. My creatinine & BUN levels, the lowest in 11 years. My B/C level lowest since 2012. Still not where I need to be, but for little more than a month? Wow! I might be wrong, but Lee Hull got CKD and decided to heavily research remedies and gave them away for others to benefit. Thank you! Update: April 28, 2019 My eGFR is now 50.

author: Steve Ingle
title: I Cured My Stage 3 Chronic Kidney Disease!
body: A year ago I was diagnosed with Stage 3 Chronic Kidney Disease. My Doctor said we should "keep an eye on it" until I either needed to go on dialysis, have a kidney transplant, or I just died! None of those options sounded too good to me, so after being shocked and stunned at this prognosis, I chose to ignore the diagnosis for the next several months. When I finally told my wife what the Dr. said, she thought I should see a specialist, so I called a Nephrologist I had been referred to. Fortunately, it took several month to get in to see her, so I started researching the internet for everything I could find on the subject myself. Miraculously, Lee was just starting to publish chapters of this book online. I started reading and practicing what he had to say, with the belief that when I got to see the Nephrologist, she would say, "I don't know why you are here, you don't have kidney disease". Well after following Lee's advice, 2 months later when it was time for my appointment, that's exactly what she said! I have been getting blood and urine tests every other month since then, and they all confirm I am free of chronic kidney disease. Now that Lee's book is available for sale, I am buying a copy to give to my Primary Care Physician next month, (you know, the one who had given me the rather dire initial prognosis). I believe every Doctor in America needs to read this book - it would save countless lives and give real hope to people with this "incurable" disease. I highly recommend it so say the least!

author: Sylvia
title: Must have resource
body: This book has more information about kidney disease treatment and kidney disease diets than any other resource or books I have found. Every kidney patient should read this book. It's sort of long, but it is written in easy to understand language, and it has a mountain of great, up-to-date information.

author: Jacob Ehrlich
title: I thought I was pretty informed until this
body: I thought I was pretty informed until I read Lee's book. I now know I knew nothing about my disease or how my kidneys worked. I have been following Lee's work for about a year, it has changed everything for me. Thank you

title: Good book
body: Great to have

title: Very technical
body: Very technical, very detailed.

title: Informative!
body: Very good book. Very informative and meticulously well researched.

title: Lovely Huge book.
body: Ok, my lovely book has just arrived and I have read the introduction only at this stage.  The author sounds amazing and describes how I was feeling when I first discovered I had stage 3 CKD.  I requested a dietician and was shocked to be told no need to change my diet.  Now, a year later my blood pressure has started to rise and once again am being told no change to diet , BP can be managed with pills.  Shocked and helpless I stumbled across this book.  I am not a big reader but I will read this book as it offers me hope and makes me feel like I am in control.  Love the quality and content so far, I will leave another review once I have finished reading but am so much happier to have the chance of doing something to improve my health.  I’m not a fan of pills.  Beautiful quality book, my pleasure to read.  Thank you Lee Hull.

title: Great research and great results !
body: This book is brilliant. So well researched and backed up correctly so that you know he’s done the work.Followed the recommendations for three months and my rGFR has gone from 50 to 57 ! Age 58.For anyone who thinks it’s long- winded.... I’m sure they’ll find dialysis pretty tedious as well. This is your life, live it !I’d LOVE some more recipes though.

title: Excellent
body: Excellent

title: Essential reading for kidney (and diabetic) patients. Buy it!
body: As a stage 5 kidney patient I really wish I had this book 8 years ago. I would pay 10x this just for the essential info about PRAL and albumin levels. (Things my GP and Nephrologists have never mentioned!!!) You don't have to read all the scientist studies, just read all the clearly written eye opening rest of it. Halt your deterioration. Extend your life. Buy this book. Amen.

title: It is a great inspiring book. I hope it works for my husband who has kidney and heart disease. Will update more once we see results.
body: Very impressive regarding research papers.Thanks Lee.Sounds like this is a ray of sunshine 🌄. Hoping to see some recepies too as part of try plan.

title: One of the most comprehensive guides to be found
body: This book is a godsend. There is so much conflicting advice out there for anyone who wants to look after their kidneys it's a mine field.  But this has so much information backed up with scientific studies and tests and no better example of how this works than from Lee himself.I have learned so much, but feel that I would like to go through all of the relevant points again and make notes and try to commit a lot to memory.I know it's a huge ask, but a book listing as many basic food products and their contents would be invaluable, it would enable myself and others to try and plan their menus around their families.Keep up the great work you're doing, educating us all one person at a time! :D

title: Brilliant
body: The book is amazing. I will now do all 8 can to survive with my kidney stage 3 disease

title: Excellent
body: Excellent

title: Excellent delivery. Book in pristine condition. Must read for anyone with Kidney issues.
body: Have as an EBook- purchased to help a newly diagnosed Kidney patient- get up the learning curve

title: Big Book
body: To cut down on shipping costs, I purchased the two books written by Lee Hull (the Food Guide and Stopping Kidney Disease).  I immediately skimmed the pages and discovered a wealth of knowledge.  This is a large book appropriate for both individuals with healthy kidneys and those who would like to learn more about CKD.  The Food Guide includes illustrations, and Lee Hull offers recipes tailored for those with chronic kidney disease (CKD). You can adjust some of the components to fit your needs if you have diabetes, liver, or heart problems.  The two books are providing you just enough advantage in your CKD journey; read with an open mind and remember that there is no one rule that fits all.

title: Most updated information's on the CKD .
body: I am in stage 3 of CKD, Bought this book couple of weeks ago, reading through half way so far ,all facts about CKD are backed by medical research and evidences , except some of the medical terminologies and research notes, which are difficult to understand by a common reader ,this book is very well written and compiled, anybody with concerns about kidneys health or CKD ,this book is a must to read,I highly appreciate and  recommend.

title: it is easy to read and you wouldnt need another book once you have this one
body: the book is greay a lot of good info

title: Excellent
body: The information in this book can be overwhelming, yet I find that it is good, well-researched information. I'm so glad I found this book when I was diagnosed with Stage 3 CKD. With a medical background, I can understand much of it, and for that I am grateful.

title: Very informative and useful, exceeds expectations
body: My grandma has chronic kidney disease and this book really helps me understand what to look out for. I've learnt a lot from this book, far more than I've imagined. The way the author explains various concepts is also easy to understand even though I don't have a science background. Thank you so much for writing this book! ❤️ I hope more people can benefit from this book and improve their kidney health. My grandma started walking for at least an hour a day for around 2 months since. Her egfr improved slightly from 14 to 18. Hopefully by following the advice in this book, we'll be able to see even more improvements. 🌞🌄💖

title: Very helpful
body: I bought it for a relative who has CKD. I read it and found the book is very inspiring and useful. it covers practically all the aspects of the disease and helps to understand all the challenges. It contains many important research information and provides new ideas and practical solutions to keep the disease under control. It also helps to avoid some serious mistakes. so it is very helpful.

title: Must read for all kidney patients
body: Very informative ,inspiring and helpful. Makes life very easy to manage for kidney patients at all stages of kidney diseases.

title: no quick and easy solutions
body: DO youever read those American websites that promise nice simple solutions to things that  then go on and on and on with waffle, and end up with the site asking you to buy a dvd course or whatever.  That's what this book is like.  It is very frustrating indeed - loads of education you don't need and haven't asked for as (in my estimation) "filler".  But if you want to know 'don't eat this' or 'do eat that', this ain't for you.

title: Tedious and difficult read
body: Tedious and repetitive. Most difficult to work through.

title: Too much and somehow not enough.
body: I never did find a “diet” plan. A great deal of unnecessary information that somehow never gets to the point. Way too many clinical trials and studies that were. Not helpful. I don’t understand my ckd any better than I did yesterday. Sorry, pass on this one.

title: It's somewhat informative but really scare tactics
body: I didn't like it at all. According to my nephrologist, most of the foods the book says are bad for you, aren't really. My Dr said it's partially due to all the supplements we consume that are not FDA aporoved!

title: Technical
body: Very technical.  I wanted information not a manual with very technical information that was interesting but hard to find the practical

title: the ink is insufficient to be able to  read the text ;  it is too light.
body: The ink in this book was not suffiecient.  Many pages the ink is so light you can not read the text.

title: Over 600 pages of tiny print and too professional got my meeds
body: I like that is a very thorough book on the topic of Kidney disease.

title: Don't bother
body: Not much help

title: Disappointing.
body: Disappointing given other reviews. Technical. No an easy read. I hope to get something from it.

title: Very small print.
body: The print is too small for me to read.
